rationale_for_baseline_tier: An anterior epistaxis that stops with correct first aid, in a haemodynamically stable patient not on anticoagulants, is self-manageable (T0): the main intervention is coaching correct technique. If bleeding is uncontrolled after 20 minutes of correct pressure, or there is haemodynamic compromise/anticoagulation, escalate to ED (T4).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Bleeding not controlled after 20 minutes of correct firm pressure, or blood continuously running down the throat despite anterior pressure
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests a posterior or refractory bleed needing cautery/packing.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the bleeding hasn't stopped after 20 minutes of correctly pinching the soft part of your nose, or blood keeps running down your throat — go to the Emergency Department (call 000 if it's very heavy).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Feeling faint/near-collapse, vomiting large amounts of blood, or a known anticoagulant with heavy bleeding
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals significant blood loss / higher-risk bleeding.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you feel faint or nearly pass out, vomit a lot of blood, or you take blood thinners and it's bleeding heavily — go to the Emergency Department promptly (call 000 if you collapse).

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Coach technique calmly; advise having a phone to hand and a plan to reach ED if uncontrolled.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Dry indoor heating and allergic rhinitis
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Humidify and treat rhinitis to reduce recurrence.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Sit up, lean forward, and firmly pinch the soft part of your nose for 10–15 minutes without letting go. It should stop. Then avoid hot drinks, hot showers, exercise and nose-blowing for 24 hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with correct first aid; ED if uncontrolled after 20 min or you feel faint
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED/000 at any time for uncontrolled bleeding or faintness.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Controlled anterior epistaxis (Kiesselbach), technique corrected; no anticoagulation/coagulopathy; stable.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring while endorsing/not correcting the head-back technique and giving no uncontrolled-bleed safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A posterior bleed continues unrecognised; the patient swallows blood, becomes hypovolaemic, and presents late.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not screening anticoagulation or haemodynamic status.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for a controllable anterior bleed that stops with correct first aid — unnecessary.
